rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARILYN ANN MALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Accounting And Disclosure Of Terms Of Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel may be asked to address the following issues: 1) Should the Court deny the petitioner's request to disclose the settlement terms as moot, in light of her 11/21/2024 declaration acknowledging receipt of a copy of the settlement agreement? 2) Should the Court deny the petitioner's request for a finding included in paragraph (d) of the proposed order that the most valuable asset of this probate was the decedent's claim to recover the Baltimore property from Mary Mallen, in light of the dismissal of that claim with prejudice filed by the current attorney of record for Daniel Mallen individually AND as administrator of this estate? 3) Should the Court order Daniel Mallen as administrator of this estate to account, and if so, should the administrator be allowed to file a single petition in the form of his first and final account from the date of death (09/05/2017) to present, as the Court has authority to order the administrator to account on its own motion under Probate Code section 10950(a) without even requiring this petition? Section 10950(b) requires the Court to order the accounting "on petition of an interested person made more than one year after issuance of letters". The petitioner is the administrator's former attorney whose fees must be paid out of the estate (see section 10831(d)). The petitioner is also assignee of Daniel Mallen's beneficial interest in this estate. Daniel Mallen's beneficial interest in this estate is assignable, unlike the related trust subject to an anti-assignment clause prohibiting the petitioner from claiming Daniel Mallen's interest in the trust as his assignee. 4) Does the administrator intend to file a corrected inventory and appraisal to remove the Baltimore property as an asset of this probate? It is unclear whether there is anything, other than the $2,781 checking accounts listed on the inventory and appraisal on file, that must be accounted for. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/31/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/20/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No GC-020 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form on the conservatee at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Allegations required by Probate Code section 1064(a)(1)-(4) must be made. See also CRC 7.575(a)(3). 3) An allegation must be made re sufficiency of bond. See LRSF 14.34(E). 4) The summary of account must be filed on the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-400(SUM). See CRC 7.575(e)(5). 5) The total amount of non-cash assets on hand at beginning of account period ($2,856,572.55) does not match the total listed on the final inventory and appraisal ($2,846,571.95). It should be noted that the final inventory and appraisal (and also the petition) listed 06/02/2022 as the date of appointment, but the beginning date of the account is 06/06/2022. It should also be noted that the corrected partial inventory and appraisal listed 06/06/2022 as the date of appointment. 6) No supplemental inventory and appraisal has been filed and served for the UPS and OMC shares reported as additional property received (Schedule 1C). See section 2613. 7) No schedule of liabilities (see section 1063(g)) was included. The fees and costs of the former court-appointed attorney for the conservatee were approved by the Court on 07/20/2023, but the schedule of disbursements does not show payment. 8) Schedule F does not show purchase of "The Charles Schwab 4%" (corporate bond) listed on Schedule E. Note: The related trust case number must be noted in parentheses on the caption page of papers subsequently filed in this conservatorship case. See LRSF 14.47(B)(3). An appearance may be required on 12/31/2024 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WARREN H. KING SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Costs As Part Of Conservatee'S Distribution In The Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether to require the petitioner-conservator or his attorney to file this fee petition in the conservatorship case in San Mateo County under Probate Code section 2640 or 2642, if the conservator's attorney's fees and costs are sought to be paid out of the conservatee spouse's estate including her share of distribution from this estate. The Court's approval of the underlying settlement was sought under sections 2501(a) and 2505(a), but not in the form of approval of a compromise of a proceeding to which a person with a disability is a party under section 3600(a)(2). The Court's approval of final distribution of this estate to the conservatee spouse was not sought as the Court's giving judgment for a person with a disability under section 3600(a)(3). Had the Court intended to characterize its order approving the settlement or final distribution as an order approving a compromise or giving judgment under section 3600(a)(2) or (3), the Court presumably would have fixed the conservator's attorney's fees and costs "as a part of" its order approving the settlement or final distribution, as required by section 3601. Unlike section 3601, section 2505 does not direct or even authorize the Court approving a section 2501 settlement petition to fix fees and costs of a conservator's attorney. The petition cites CCP section 372 & CRC 7.955 in support of the Court's authority to fix the conservator's attorney's fees and costs in this case, but see CCP section 372(a)(3) requiring compliance with sections 3600 et seq. and CRC 7.955 advisory committee comment requiring the Court to approve the attorney's fees under section 3601. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF R. GENE GEISLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The 4th amended petition contains the following procedural defects: a. Relationships to Decedent were not provided for those in category 1 in Attachment 8. b. Item 2b is incomplete (name of proposed personal representative). c. The caption and body of petition is inconsistent: Item 2b requests appointment of administrator with will annexed but the caption of the petition does not. In addition, the caption of the petition requests both letters testamentary and letters of administration. d. Item 5a is incomplete (whether Decedent was survived by a registered domestic partner). e. Notice: i. Item 1 of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate filed on 9/6/24 and 9/11/24 is missing (name of decedent). ii. Item 3 of the notice does not specify that a 4th Amended Petition has been filed. 2) Petitioner states that he is not requesting admission of the 1st codicil and a holographic instrument (which would have been the 2nd codicil) because it has not been located and because it was "expressly replaced by Paragraph Third of the Third Codicil". However, Paragraph Third does not revoke any prior instruments, and Petitioner does not allege why he is not requesting admission of lost codicils. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROGER HENRY PEDEUPE REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED 5/23/18, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee; Determining The Validity Of Trust Provisions; And F
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The issues have not been fully cleared: (1) Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to the settlor/current beneficiary, Roger Henry Pedeupe. (2) Petitioner supplemented the family tree which reveals more living heirs. However, the paternal side 7 living first cousins twice removed and the material side 10 living first cousins once removed have not been noticed. (3) Need allegation why the Personal Property Gift Letter (dated 7/6/21) cannot/should not be considered as the Will, especially the document itself sets forth so on its face. Prob. Code sec. 6110(c)(2). (4) Petitioner supplemented providing more supporting information. He says the court may appoint a GAL; however, the request be made from petitioner. (5) Further supporting allegation regarding the Gift Letter. For example, who prepared the document, if the preparer has any further supporting allegation. (6) The fair market value of the trust asset for the full bond. Prob. Code sec. 15602(a)(3). LR 14.16.G. Petition alleges that the real property is the trust asset; however, seeking the minimum bond only. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCIS-JOSEPH MASSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 5/27/10. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. (NB: Instructions for remote appearance should be attached to the NOTICE, not to a petition.) Status hearing will be set for 12/16/2025. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 12/16/2025, no appearance will be required on that date.
